1.11a: Contact the branch office that issued the instructions.
1.11b: Contact the branch office that issued the instructions.
1.7: Lending is permitted when the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
Contact the instructing branch for specific instructions in these cases.
1.14: The firm may act if the fee earner or their family is the seller, provided a separate fee earner of equal or higher standing within the firm handles the transaction for the lender.
The fee earner representing the lender must not be the seller or related to the seller.
1.15: The firm may act if the fee earner or their family is the borrower, provided a separate fee earner of equal or higher standing within the firm handles the transaction for the lender.
The fee earner representing the lender must not be the borrower or related to the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's conveyancer details is not required.
The acting conveyancer must verify the identity of the seller's conveyancer.
3.1.4: Contact the instructing branch if the seller is not legally represented.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents include a national or employer identity card with signature and photograph, or a full UK driving licence.
3.2.3: Details of the seller's conveyancer are only required upon specific request.
3.2.4: Contact the instructing branch if the seller is not legally represented.
4.5a: Contact the instructing branch for re-inspection requests.
4.5b: Send the certificate of title to the instructing branch office.
4.1: A valuation report is usually provided by the lender.
4.3: Contact the instructing branch if the valuer's assumptions are found to be incorrect.
5.1.1: Contact the instructing branch if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.2.1: Contact the instructing branch if the seller is not the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports must be submitted only if they indicate a need for further investigation or affect the security.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the search agent has adequate professional indemnity insurance.
The conveyancer must be able to provide a clear Certificate of Title.
5.4.6: Search insurance may be accepted in lieu of searches with branch approval.
Conveyancer must confirm in writing that the insurance adequately protects the lender.
The policy must be assignable and cover successors in title.
Title must be certified as good and marketable with an unqualified Certificate of Title.
Search insurance is not accepted for Land Registry searches.
Borrowers must be informed of the risks of using insurance over searches.
5.5.3a: Report to the branch if breaches or conditions will not be satisfied by completion.
5.5.3b: Lender requires an original or copy of the planning permission.
5.5.3c: Lender requires an original or copy of building regulation consents.
5.5.3d: Lender requires certificates of lawful use or development/established use certificates.
5.5.4: Contact the instructing branch regarding property restrictions affecting value or marketability.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted subject to branch approval and compliance with paragraph 5.7.2.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.7.1c: Report freehold flats or flying freeholds to the instructing branch.
5.8.1: Lending allowed for buildings with up to four flats if the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold.
A charge is required on the freehold title.
Arrangements must be reported to the branch if not covered in the valuation report.
5.8.5: Reciprocal leasehold/freehold arrangements for two-flat buildings are accepted.
Lender requires a first charge over both the freehold and leasehold titles.
5.9.1: Commonhold is accepted; notify the branch as early as possible.
5.10.1: Contact the instructing branch regarding restrictions on use.
5.13.1: Clear bankruptcy searches must be obtained for all parties contributing to the purchase price balance.
Notify the lender if clear searches cannot be obtained.
5.14.15a: Contact the instructing branch if the landlord is absentee or insolvent.
5.14.15b: Comprehensive indemnity insurance is accepted for absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.14.1: Minimum lease term must be the mortgage term plus 50 years.
Notify the lender if the lease is shorter than 85 years and not noted in the valuation report.
5.14.9: Ground rent and fees must be reasonable throughout the lease term.
Onerous ground rent provisions must be reported.
RPI-linked increases are generally acceptable if not compounded.
Increases linked to indexes greater than RPI or unreasonable multipliers are not acceptable.
Provisions likely to create an Assured Shorthold Tenancy (AST) under the Housing Act 1988 are not acceptable.
Contact the branch to confirm if an indemnity policy or lease variation is acceptable for AST risks.
5.14.10: Contact the instructing branch for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms if it meets Part 1 paragraph 9 requirements.
5.14.12: Send clear ground rent and service charge receipts to the lender.
5.14.13: Send receipted copies of notices or evidence of service to the lender.
5.15.2a: Contact the branch for management company issues.
Estate rent charges are acceptable if section 121 Law of Property Act 1925 is excluded, OR there is a 28-day notice mortgagee protection clause, OR the management company is resident-owned.
A deed of variation is required if these criteria are not met; indemnity insurance is not accepted.
5.15.2b: Send management company share certificates after completion.
5.15.2c: Send signed blank stock transfer forms after completion.
5.15.2d: Send management company memorandum and articles of association after completion.
5.16.2: Contact the branch if unable to certify a search entry does not relate to the borrower.
5.17.5a: Send a certified copy of the power of attorney after completion.
5.17.5b: Send a statutory declaration of non-revocation for power of attorney after completion.
5.19.1: Contact the branch for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership schemes.
5.20.1: Report if solar PV roof space leases fail to meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: Solar PV lease break clauses must state the provider is responsible for removal costs and damage repair upon lender termination.
5.20.4: Disclose details of existing Green Deal Plans.
6.1.3: Contact the branch if the borrower does not take up the mortgage offer.
6.2.1: Contact the branch for property description discrepancies.
6.3.1: Contact the branch for issues relating to the purchase price.
6.4.4: Report all incentives to the lender.
6.4.5: Contact the branch if the conveyancer will not control the payment of all purchase money.
6.5.1: Contact the branch if vacant possession will not be provided.
6.6.1: Contact the branch for letting/buy-to-let inquiries.
6.6.2: Contact the branch if letting details are unknown at completion.
6.6.3: Send counterpart or certified copies of tenancy agreements after completion.
6.6.4: HMOs are accepted on a buy-to-let basis for up to 6 bedrooms.
Requires planning permission or established use certificate.
Requires valid HMO license or council confirmation if not required; license must be in place by completion.
Tenants must be jointly and severally liable under a single agreement.
Property must meet legal bedroom size requirements.
6.7.1: Acceptable 10-year warranties: NHBC (including Solo), Zurich Municipal (including Self Build), Premier Guarantee (no reservations), Building Life Plans (subject to sum insured confirmation), Buildzone.
Warranties must cover common parts for flats.
Other schemes considered on merit via the branch.
6.7.2: Send copies of warranty certificates and guarantees to the lender after completion.
Originals must be sent to the borrower.
6.7.3: Send assignments of building standards indemnity schemes to the lender.
6.7.4: If no warranty exists, work must be monitored by a professional (excluding FASI, MASI, or AssocRICS).
Requires CML Professional Consultant’s Certificate issued before exchange.
Requires Architects certificate for borrower benefit, building regulation sign-off, and PI insurance.
For flats, the certificate must cover common parts.
6.7.6: Send the professional consultant's certificate to the lender after completion.
6.8.1: Contact the branch if there is no agreement and bond for unadopted roads or sewers.
6.9.1: Contact the branch if necessary easements are missing.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments are released to the borrower or solicitor.
6.11.1: Contact the branch if the property is affected by redevelopment or road proposals.
6.12.1: Contact the branch regarding pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options.
6.13.1: Contact the branch if the property is affected by an undischarged improvement or repair grant.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required from all occupants aged 17 or over, excluding children in full-time education living away from home.
7.4: Contact the branch if accuracy of disclosed information is doubted.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Send indemnity insurance policies to the lender after completion.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the higher of the purchase price or 110% of the mortgage advance.
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance is 5 working days.
10.3: Standard deductions are detailed in the offer documentation.
10.7: Contact the branch for return instructions on delayed completions.
Advances must be returned via CHAPS quoting borrower name and account number.
10.9: Contact the instructing branch if completion is delayed.
10.10: Contact the branch for case-by-case instructions on holding the advance.
10.11: Interest on delayed returns is charged at the agreed mortgage rate.
12.3.1: Contact the branch for release of retentions or instalments.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is required for retentions, instalments, current account mortgages, or secured overdrafts.
14.1.5: Send the original mortgage deed and all original title documents to the lender.
14.2.1: Send title deeds and documents to the instructing branch office.
16.1.1: Contact the instructing branch for title documents.
16.3.7b: The attestation clause must be signed for and on behalf of the Bank by two Authorised Signatories and dated.
16.3.1: Lender has no standard form of transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.2: Contact the branch for debt amount information.
16.3.4: A reconstitution of security is required for transfers of equity; the transfer deed is not sent to the lender.
16.4.1: Contact the branch for consent to let applications.
16.4.2: BTL lettings must be ASTs; copies are not required if they are valid ASTs. Refer to the offer for other letting permissions.
16.5.3a: Send deeds of variation to the instructing branch.
16.5.3b: Send deeds of rectification to the instructing branch.
16.5.3c: Send deeds of easement to the instructing branch.
16.5.3d: Send option agreements to the instructing branch.
16.5.2: Contact the branch to confirm that deeds or agreements will not affect the security.
17.1.1: Contact the instructing branch for redemption statements.
17.2.1a: Send discharge and repayment remittance to the instructing branch.
17.2.1b: Lender issues discharge via form DS1.
